description:
You are a SOC (Security-Operations-Center) analyst who has just been handed two
    SSH authentication logs that already live on the system.  
    Your job is to examine every log line that contains the exact phrase

        Failed password

    and then create two artefacts in a brand-new directory.

    1. Create the directory “/home/user/analysis”.  
       (The directory does **not** exist yet.)

    2. Inside that directory create the CSV file
          /home/user/analysis/failed_login_summary.csv
       having **exactly** this comma-separated, four-line layout:

          file,total_failed,unique_ip_count
          auth.log.2023-07-21,5,2
          auth.log.2023-07-22,6,3

       Formatting rules the automated tests will enforce:
         • The first line is a header line and must match the header shown above  
           (all lowercase, exactly three comma-separated column names, no spaces).  
         • Each subsequent line must start with the log-file name, followed by the
           total number of “Failed password” lines found in that file, followed by
           the count of **distinct** source IPs that generated those failures.  
         • Columns are separated by a single comma, with NO space after the comma.  
         • The two data lines must appear in **alphabetical order** by file name
           (auth.log.2023-07-21 comes before auth.log.2023-07-22).  
         • The file must finish with a single newline (Unix “\n”) at the end.

    3. Still in “/home/user/analysis”, create the text file
          /home/user/analysis/suspicious_ips.txt
       that lists every IP address which generated **five (5) or more** failed
       log-in attempts **across all logs combined**.  

       Formatting rules for suspicious_ips.txt:
         • One IP address per line.  
         • Lines sorted in ascending (lexicographical) order.  
         • The file must also finish with a single newline.  
         • Based on the supplied logs, the finished file must contain **exactly**
           the two lines shown below:  

             10.0.0.5  
             192.168.1.10

    You should feel free to use whatever shell tools you like (grep, awk, sort,
    uniq, etc. or even a small script) but the two files above must be produced
    exactly as specified so the automated grader can verify them byte-for-byte.
truth:
<!--
        PRE-EXISTING FILES & THEIR CONTENTS
        These log files are already present when the agent session starts.
    -->
    <log dir="/home/user/syslogs">
        <file name="auth.log.2023-07-21"><![CDATA[
Jul 21 10:03:12 server sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54321 ssh2
Jul 21 10:05:44 server sshd[12346]: Accepted password for user1 from 192.168.1.3 port 54322 ssh2
Jul 21 11:15:01 server sshd[12347]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 60001 ssh2
Jul 21 11:16:10 server sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user test from 192.168.1.10 port 60002 ssh2
Jul 21 12:00:00 server cron[12349]: (root) CMD (some job)
Jul 21 12:10:55 server sshd[12350]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.10 port 60003 ssh2
Jul 21 13:15:23 server sshd[12351]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 10.0.0.5 port 60004 ssh2
]]></file>

        <file name="auth.log.2023-07-22"><![CDATA[
Jul 22 09:00:22 server sshd[22345]: Failed password for root from 10.0.0.5 port 60101 ssh2
Jul 22 09:02:13 server sshd[22346]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 10.0.0.5 port 60102 ssh2
Jul 22 09:05:44 server sshd[22347]: Accepted password for user2 from 192.168.1.3 port 60103 ssh2
Jul 22 10:15:01 server sshd[22348]: Failed password for root from 8.8.8.8 port 60104 ssh2
Jul 22 10:25:30 server sshd[22349]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 10.0.0.5 port 60105 ssh2
Jul 22 11:45:10 server sshd[22350]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.10 port 60106 ssh2
Jul 22 12:30:22 server sshd[22351]: Failed password for root from 192.168.1.10 port 60107 ssh2
]]></file>
    </log>

    <!--
        EXPECTED OUTPUT FILES & THEIR EXACT CONTENTS
        The grader will look for these after the agent completes the task.
    -->
    <expected dir="/home/user/analysis">
        <file name="failed_login_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
file,total_failed,unique_ip_count
auth.log.2023-07-21,5,2
auth.log.2023-07-22,6,3
]]></file>

        <file name="suspicious_ips.txt"><![CDATA[
10.0.0.5
192.168.1.10
]]></file>
    </expected>
